title: PMArtifactBase
description: Shared base shape for every PM-flavor agent artifact (status updates, coordinator artifacts, PM analyses, comms drafts, knowledge entries). Per-agent schemas extend this via $ref + a metadata sub-schema. The Documents workspace renders any artifact uniformly: title -> summary -> body_markdown -> type-specific metadata.

artifact_type.description: Discriminator for which PM agent produced this artifact. Per-agent schemas restrict this enum to a single value.

artifact_id.description: Stable identifier (UUID v4 or kebab-case slug) assigned by the runtime. Reused across revisions only via parent_id; each new draft gets a fresh artifact_id.

parent_id.description: If this artifact supersedes a prior draft, the prior artifact_id. Null for fresh drafts.

title.description: Human-facing title rendered in the Documents tab.

summary.description: One-paragraph TL;DR. Used in list views and Telegram notifications.

body_markdown.description: Full artifact body as GitHub-flavored Markdown. Renderable as-is in the Documents tab and exportable to Drive.

metadata.description: Type-specific structured fields. Per-agent schemas tighten this with their own required keys; the base accepts any object.

suggested_distribution.description: Recommended recipient roles or email addresses (e.g. 'owner_pm', 'gc_super', 'charles@...'). Null when no distribution is appropriate (internal-only artifacts).

citations.description: Sources backing the claims in the artifact. Required even if the agent's only inputs were prior approved artifacts (cite those).

confidence.description: Self-reported confidence in [0.0, 1.0]. Below 0.70 forces tier-0 mandatory review regardless of trust_tier_default.

escalation_reasons.description: Short tags describing why this artifact needs attention. Routing logic reads these to bump trust tier or attach reviewers.

citation.description: A single source backing a claim in the artifact. 'kind' (preferred) or 'purpose' (alias) identifies the source class; 'ref' is the human-readable pointer; 'note' is optional disambiguation. At least one of 'kind' or 'purpose' must be present so downstream consumers always have a descriptor; both are accepted to match natural agent output.

kind.description: Canonical source class. Conventional values listed for routing consistency, but free-form values are accepted so schema validation catches real bugs rather than prompt-style nuance.

purpose.description: Free-form purpose-style alias for 'kind' (e.g., 'rate_source', 'quantity_basis', 'design_input'). Accepted because some agents naturally emit 'purpose' instead of 'kind'; consumers should treat 'purpose' as semantically equivalent to 'kind'.

ref.description: Pointer to the source: spec section number, drawing sheet, RFI ID, prior artifact_id, etc. Must exist in the input context; never invented.

note.description: Optional short note on what this citation supports (which claim, which paragraph).

escalation_tag.description: Short, machine-friendly tag. Conventional values are listed for routing consistency, but free-form tags are allowed.
